prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, small, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Phone split into country code select and local number input in separate wrappers Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Required: Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input Required: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Reference DOM (Conditional radio reveal): Selecting Yes on sponsorship reveals follow-up textarea Reference DOM (Title attribute tooltip label): Field identified primarily via title attribute on input with minimal label Reference DOM (CSS hidden wizard steps): Multi-step wizard with only one step visible via CSS class toggling Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: split-phone, aria-labelledby-hidden, div-role-textbox, readonly-prefill, iframe-apply. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: two-column grid without card wrapper. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Conditional radio reveal; Title attribute tooltip label; CSS hidden wizard steps.
